nonworking_or_blocked_routes.notes: The external agent reported that the childhood obesity meta-analysis page and PDF include only article text and figures, with no supplementary Excel/CSV datasets or data tables.
The external agent reported that Cambridge's repository page for the adult obesity meta-analysis lists the published PDF and bibliographic metadata, with no extraction workbook or data file.

The external agent reported that the 2024 preprint 'Are the Risk of Generalizability Biases Generalizable?' lists supplementary DOCX files with narrative appendices/search strategies only, and that the data availability statement says data are available on reasonable request to the corresponding author.

The external agent reported that the dissertation 'Causes and Consequences of the Risk of Generalizability Biases in Health Behavioral Interventions' is downloadable as a PDF, but has no accompanying data files or extraction tables.

dn_assessment.notes: Across the examined article, preprint, repository, and dissertation routes, no publicly downloadable row-level dataset comparing pilot/preliminary studies with larger efficacy/effectiveness trials was found. Published materials appear to provide narrative summaries and meta-analysis results, not the underlying extraction workbook.

codex_local_followup.status: No local D/N workbook to mine.

recommended_next_action: Contact the corresponding authors of the childhood obesity, adult obesity, and broader health-behavior RGB studies, or the dissertation author, and request the row-level extraction dataset with preliminary/pilot SMDs, larger-trial SMDs, original/preliminary N, larger-trial N, construct/outcome labels, and source-study identifiers.
